title: Intro 1
description: Welcome to Rustlings! This first exercise is a simple introduction to get you started. Your task is to make the program print a greeting.
instructions: Remove the `I AM NOT DONE` comment and make the code compile by completing the println! macro call.
hints: The println! macro uses `{}` as a placeholder for values you pass as arguments.
You need to provide a string or value after the comma inside println! to fill the `{}` placeholder.
Try adding a string literal like "world" as the second argument to println!
explanation: The println! macro in Rust is used for printing to the console. The `{}` syntax is a placeholder that gets replaced by the value of the argument that follows the format string. This is similar to format strings in other languages.
